varieties.name: Clemson Spineless 🏆
varieties.traits: 4-5 feet tall, dark green, spineless pods. Uniform growth. Open-pollinated heirloom.
varieties.yield_notes: Heavy producer, 12-15 t/ha. Continuous harvest 10-12 weeks.
varieties.availability: Widely available: The Seed Collection (Australia), Bunnings, Yates, Queensland nurseries. Most popular variety in Australia.

varieties.name: Burgundy 🏆
varieties.traits: 3-4 feet tall. Burgundy-red stems and pods. Green leaves contrast with red stems. Ornamental.
varieties.availability: The Seed Collection (Australia), online specialty suppliers

varieties.name: Louisiana Green Velvet 🏆
varieties.traits: Award-winning. Tall, vigorous. Developed for high heat and humidity.

varieties.name: Annie Oakley
varieties.origin: USA hybrid (modern improvement of Clemson Spineless)
varieties.traits: Dwarf/compact, 4 feet. Dark green. Spineless, extra slender shape.

varieties.name: Emerald 🏆
varieties.origin: Campbell's Soup Company, USA, 1950s. Heirloom, open-pollinated
varieties.traits: Tall 6-8 feet. Dark green. Round, smooth pods (not ridged).
varieties.availability: Specialty heirloom seed suppliers in Australia

varieties.name: Gold Coast
varieties.traits: Compact to 5 feet. Well-branched. Drought tolerant.

varieties.name: Cajun Delight
varieties.traits: Compact 4 feet. Award-winning. Spineless.

varieties.name: Star of David 🏆
varieties.traits: Medium height. Known for distinctive pod shape. Spines on pod tips.

varieties.name: Perkins Long Pod 🏆
varieties.traits: Traditional heirloom with long pods. Reliable producer.

varieties.name: Cow Horn 🏆
varieties.traits: Giant 7-8 feet tall. Impressive stature.

varieties.name: Silver Queen 🏆
varieties.traits: Large plants 6 feet tall. Southern belle variety.

varieties.name: Red Velvet 🏆
varieties.traits: Crimson red stems, 4-5 feet. Ornamental and productive.

varieties.name: Blondy
varieties.traits: Grows to 5 feet. Named for pale green pods. Early maturing.

varieties.name: Baby Bubba
varieties.traits: Compact 3-4 feet tall, 24-inch diameter. Perfect for containers.

varieties.name: Arka Anamika
varieties.origin: IIHR Bangalore, India, 1994 (replacement for Pusa Sawani)
varieties.traits: Dark green, sparse pigmentation. Single stem, short internodes (2-4cm). Broad leaves. Resistant to YVMV.
varieties.yield_notes: 10-12 t/ha summer, higher in kharif. 21-24 t/ha potential.
varieties.availability: Kerala agricultural stores, IIHR, government seed distribution

varieties.name: Susthira 🌱
varieties.origin: Kerala Agricultural University (KAU), India - INDIGENOUS Kerala development. Uses Abelmoschus caillei genetics.
varieties.traits: YVMV resistant specifically developed for Kerala. Purple leaf veins.

varieties.name: Arka Abhay
varieties.origin: IIHR Bangalore, India (sister line of Arka Anamika)
varieties.traits: Resembles Arka Anamika. Tall, well-branched. YVMV resistant. Tolerant to fruit borer.

varieties.name: Varsha Uphar
varieties.origin: HAU Hisar, India, 1996 (Lam selection 1 × Parbhani Kranti) - specifically bred for rainy season
varieties.traits: Medium tall, short internodes. 3-4 branches. Green foliage, occasionally pigmented petioles.
varieties.availability: Widely available in India, Kerala agricultural stores

varieties.name: Parbhani Kranti
varieties.origin: Marathwada Agricultural University, Parbhani, India, 1986 (A. esculentus cv. Pusa Sawani × A. manihot ssp. manihot)
varieties.traits: Single stem, tall. Dark green foliage. First fruit 5-6th node. Interspecific hybrid.
varieties.availability: Widely available across India, Kerala agricultural stores

varieties.name: CO-3
varieties.origin: TNAU Coimbatore, India (Parbhani Kranti × MDU-1) - F1 hybrid
varieties.traits: Vigorous hybrid. Improved over CO-2.
varieties.availability: TNAU, Tamil Nadu and Kerala seed suppliers

varieties.name: COBhH 1
varieties.origin: HC & RI, TNAU Coimbatore, India (Varsha Uphar × Pusa A-4) - Recent hybrid
varieties.traits: Modern hybrid with disease resistance.
varieties.availability: TNAU, Tamil Nadu and Kerala agricultural stores

varieties.name: Pusa Sawani
varieties.origin: IARI New Delhi, India, 1960s (IC-1542 × Pusa Makhmali)
varieties.traits: Plants compact with close nodes. Dark green foliage. Once most popular in India.
varieties.availability: Widely available from Kerala agricultural stores, government seed centers

varieties.name: Kashi Pragati
varieties.traits: Average 2 branches per plant. Flowering at 4th node after 36-38 days rainy season. Highly tolerant to YVMV.

varieties.name: CO-1 (Aruna)
varieties.origin: TNAU Coimbatore, India, 1976 (selection from Red Wonder, Hyderabad)
varieties.traits: Medium tall (90-120cm) with 5-8 branches. Scarlet red stem, shoots, petioles. Deeply lobed leaves (5-7 lobes). Ornamental.

varieties.name: Punjab Padmini
varieties.origin: PAU Ludhiana, India (A. esculentus × A. manihot ssp. manihot)
varieties.traits: Rapid growth reaching 180-200cm. Pigmentation on stem, shoots, petiole, basal lower veins.

varieties.name: Hisar Unnat
varieties.traits: Medium tall with short internodes. 3-4 branches. Field tolerance to leaf hopper.

varieties.name: Pusa A-4
varieties.traits: Early maturing variety. Compact plants.
varieties.availability: IARI, widely available in India including Kerala
